title: Synthetic 'messy' clinical data-cleaning dataset + exercises
context: Data cleaning is a foundational skill for any cancer data analyst: real clinical datasets are notoriously messy (inconsistent codings, missing values, outliers, free-text fields). Teaching this skill requires a realistic 'messy' dataset that learners can clean, but real clinical data is controlled-access. A synthetic 'messy' clinical dataset — deliberately introduced encoding errors, missing value patterns, inconsistent categorical codings, and outlier values calibrated to realistic distributions — teaches this skill safely. The exercises are embedded in the deliverable (unlike the expression task which has a separate lesson task). Depends on catalog-010 (calibration source approved), synthesis-004, and tooling-006.
objective: Produce a synthetic 'messy' clinical data-cleaning dataset with deliberately introduced data quality issues, full documentation, gate PASS artifact, Zenodo DOI, and embedded exercises teaching systematic data cleaning.
acceptanceCriteria: Synthetic clinical tabular dataset generated from a seed + provenanced parameters; includes deliberate data quality issues: missing values, inconsistent categorical codings, outlier values, and at least one structural issue (e.g. mixed date formats).
Data quality issues are documented in the data dictionary with a 'clean version' reference so learners can verify their cleaning result.
gate-002 PASS artifact committed; safety notice present; riskTier:low; no individual-level data used as input.
Completeness >= 90/100; Croissant metadata valid; Zenodo DOI minted; reproducibility check = PASS.
At least 2 embedded exercises (cleaning tasks) with worked solutions, structured using the autograde-012 framework where available.
outputLicense: CC0-1.0; safety reviewer and technical reviewer sign-off.
output: A synthetic 'messy' clinical data-cleaning dataset CSV/TSV + clean reference CSV + provenance.json + gate PASS artifact + Croissant metadata + data dictionary + embedded exercises/solutions + Zenodo DOI.
